Gossypium is a plant species commonly referred to as cotton, a shrub native to tropical and subtropical regions of Africa and Asia. The health benefits of Gossypium include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to help in reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Gossypium include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Gossypium is commonly used in herbal preparations such as cottonseed oil, which is used to treat skin conditions, and cottonroot bark extract, which is used to treat conditions such as fever and rheumatism.
